inflection_points.description: First reference to new romantic partner in messages
inflection_points.evidence: Row 215 — "She gets all mad if you ask" (partner-related context)

inflection_points.description: First possible legal/court reference in messages
inflection_points.significance: Early legal awareness — needs CP1 confirmation
inflection_points.note: Keyword match may be incidental — Eli should confirm actual context

inflection_points.description: Major communication density spike — 14-day avg: 15.0 → 28.6 msg/day
inflection_points.evidence: Batch B-017 (582 msgs, Jul-Aug 2024) is one of the highest-density batches
inflection_points.significance: Significant escalation event — likely a major conflict or triggering event; corresponds to early conflict period
Second escalation period — two major spikes within 2 months

inflection_points.description: Sharp density drop: 30.5 → 5.9 msg/day (sudden de-escalation or silence)
inflection_points.evidence: Rapid transition from high-conflict Oct period to relative quiet
inflection_points.significance: Possible court date, legal intervention, or external event causing communication reduction

inflection_points.description: Density spike: 5.0 → 10.1 msg/day (resumption after Oct drop)
inflection_points.evidence: Communication resumes at moderate level after Oct silence
inflection_points.significance: Pattern of escalation, sudden silence, resumption — possible legal timeline correlation

inflection_points.description: LARGEST density spike in dataset: 12.9 → 37.0 msg/day
inflection_points.evidence: Batch B-025 (501 msgs, May-Jun 2025) spans Phase A/B boundary; this spike triggers near Phase B start
inflection_points.significance: Major escalation event at or near start of Phase B (Eli's early-awareness period); likely significant triggering event

inflection_points.description: Density drop after May spike: 39.1 → 16.2 msg/day
inflection_points.evidence: Moderate de-escalation following the Phase B transition spike
inflection_points.significance: Pattern mirrors Oct 2024 — spike followed by partial pullback

inflection_points.description: Density spike in Phase B: 10.1 → 21.2 msg/day
inflection_points.evidence: Batch B-027 (503 msgs, Jul-Aug 2025) shows elevated conflict ratio
inflection_points.significance: Phase B escalation — during period when Eli was actively applying new communication strategies

RF-13.notes: High signal (125 hits); run LAST Tier 1 per RC-PIPELINE — needs full cross-RF picture

notes: RF-10 (Refusal to Co-Parent) keyword score is artificially LOW — this RF requires gap/response-pattern analysis, not keywords. Upgraded to full pass in priority matrix.
RF-11 (Inappropriate Child Involvement) keyword patterns underestimate true signal — context-dependent flagging requires primary pass.
RF-12 (Strategic Communication) is low by keywords but potentially high by behavioral analysis — run in Tier 1 per RC-PIPELINE rule.
RF-05 keyword scan excluded all-caps pattern which was matching media filename references (3,001 media messages in dataset).
3,001 media messages (IMAGE/VIDEO/AUDIO) counted in episode structure but content not analyzed per protocol.
DIRECTION NOTE: Melissa sends 71.2% of messages (11,107 vs 4,492). This skew should be factored into RF interpretation.

analyst_observations: Phase A covers 82% of messages over 3.3 years (Jan 2022 - May 2025). Phase B and C cover 10% and 7% respectively, representing only 18 months total. Phase A will dominate all pass outputs.
Two major density spikes in 2024 (Aug and Oct) and one massive spike in May 2025 (37 msg/day, the dataset peak) suggest three significant escalation events. Eli should contextualize these.
The Oct 2024 spike-and-drop pattern (density went from 30 to 6 msg/day in 2 weeks) is unusual and suggests a possible external intervention, legal filing, or significant boundary event.
RF-01 (Parental Alienation) shows very strong Received dominance (62/66 hits from Melissa). If keyword scanning is representative, alienation-related language is heavily unidirectional.

RF-07 (Boundary Violations) shows meaningful Sent signal (39/111) — this is an important finding that both parties show boundary-related language. Primary pass will distinguish legitimate boundary-setting from violations.
RF-09 (Gaslighting) shows relatively balanced Sent/Received (20/17 by keywords) — this unusual bidirectional pattern warrants careful analysis. May reflect defensive denials on both sides.
B-031 is very small (61 messages, 4 days of data through Apr 12 2026) — may want to verify this is truly the most recent data.
